description_long: This house features a steeply pitched gable roof with a reddish-brown color and visible shingle details. The exterior walls are light gray with a timber-frame pattern, evoking a traditional half-timbered architectural style. The base of the house is a solid blue-gray foundation, with a few scattered stone details. The structure is rectangular, with the gable ends forming the narrower sides and the longer sides covered by the roof. There are no visible windows or doors, giving the facade a simple, stylized appearance. The roof includes small raised elements, possibly representing vents or minor roof features.
description: A stylized, half-timbered house with a steep reddish-brown gable roof and light gray walls, set on a blue-gray foundation.
description_view_0: Narrow gable end with timber-frame pattern, no visible windows or doors, and a steep roof peak.
description_view_1: Long side of the house with a broad roof surface, timber-frame pattern, and small roof details.
description_view_2: Opposite gable end, similar to view 0, with matching timber-frame pattern and foundation stones.
description_view_3: Opposite long side, showing the full length of the roof and foundation, with minor stone details.
attribute_entryway_design: No visible entryway; facade is plain with timber framing and no door or porch features.
